heading: Documents to be published in Federal Register
label: 1505 Documents to be published in Federal Register
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on 44 U.S. Code, 1964, ed., § 305 (, ; , ).July 26, 1935, ch. 417, § 549 Stat. 501June 25, 1956, ch. 44470 Stat. 337

Ex. Ord. No. 11030. Preparation, Presentation, Filing, and Publication of Executive Orders and ProclamationsEx. Ord. No. 11030, , 27 F.R. 5847, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 11354, , 32 F.R. 7695; Ex. Ord. No. 12080, , 43 F.R. 42235; Ex. Ord. No. 12608, , 52 F.R. 34617; Ex. Ord. No. 13403, § 1, , 71 F.R. 28543; Ex. Ord. No. 13683, § 1, , 79 F.R. 75041, provided:June 19, 1962May 23, 1967Sept. 18, 1978Sept. 9, 1987May 12, 2006Dec. 11, 2014By virtue of the authority vested in me by the Federal Register Act (, as amended; .) [now this chapter], and as President of the United States, I hereby prescribe the following regulations governing the preparation, presentation, filing, and publication of Executive orders and proclamations:49 Stat. 50044 U.S.C. 301et seq1. . Proposed Executive orders and proclamations shall be prepared in accordance with the following requirements:SectionForm(a) The order or proclamation shall be given a suitable title.(b) The order or proclamation shall contain a citation of the authority under which it is issued.(c) Punctuation, capitalization, spelling, and other matters of style shall, in general, conform to the most recent edition of the Style Manual of the United States Government Printing Office.(d) The spelling of geographic names shall conform to the decisions of the Board on Geographic Names, established by , ().Section 2 of the Act of July 25, 194761 Stat. 45643 U.S.C. 364a(e) Descriptions of tracts of land shall conform, so far as practicable, to the most recent edition of the “Specifications for Descriptions of Tracts of Land for Use in Executive Orders and Proclamations,” prepared by the Bureau of Land Management, Department of the Interior.(f) Proposed Executive orders and proclamations shall be prepared on paper approximately 8.5 × 14 inches, shall have a left-hand margin of approximately 1 inch and a right-hand margin of approximately 1 inch, and shall be double-spaced, except that quotations, tabulations, and descriptions of land may be single-spaced.(g) Proclamations issued by the President shall conclude with the following described recitation—IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand this ________ day of ________, in the year of our lord ________, and of the Independence of the United States of America, the ________.. 2. . (a) A proposed Executive order or proclamation shall first be submitted to the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, together with a letter, signed by the head or other properly authorized officer of the originating Federal agency, explaining the nature, purpose, background, and effect of the proposed Executive order or proclamation and its relationship, if any, to pertinent laws and other Executive orders or proclamations.SecRouting and approval of drafts(b) If the Director of the Office of Management and Budget approves the proposed Executive order or proclamation, he shall transmit it to the Attorney General for his consideration as to both form and legality.(c) If the proposed Executive order or proclamation is disapproved by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget or by the Attorney General, it shall not thereafter be presented to the President unless it is accompanied by a statement of the reasons for such disapproval.. 3. . (a) If the order or proclamation is signed by the President, the original and two copies thereof shall be forwarded to the Director of the Office of the Federal Register for publication in theSecFederal Register.Routing and certification of originals and copies(b) The Office of the Federal Register shall cause to be placed upon the copies of all Executive orders and proclamations forwarded as provided in subsection (a) of this section the following notation, to be signed by the Director or by some person authorized by him to sign such notation: “Certified to be a true copy of the original.”. 4. . Except as may be otherwise provided by law, responsibility for the preparation and presentation of proposed proclamations calling for the observance of special days, or other periods of time, or events shall be assigned by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to such agencies or offices as the Director may consider appropriate. Such proposed proclamations shall be submitted to the Director, or to an office within the Executive Office of the President designated by the Director, at least sixty days before the date of the specified observance. Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 2, the Director or the head of such designated office, as appropriate, shall transmit any approved commemorative proclamations to the President.SecProclamations calling for the observance of special days or events. 5. . (a) Proclamations to be issued under the Trade Act of 1974 or other trade law (“trade proclamations”) shall be prepared by the United States Trade Representative and submitted to the Attorney General for consideration as to both form and legality. Section 2 of this order does not apply to trade proclamations.SecTrade Proclamations. 6. . Consonant with the provisions of , United States Code ( [sic]; ), nothing in this order shall be construed to apply to treaties, conventions, protocols, or other international agreements, or proclamations thereof by the President.SecProclamations of treaties excludedsection 1511 of title 4449 Stat. 50344 U.S.C. 1511. 7. . The term “Presidential proclamations and Executive orders,” as used in sub, United States Code (), shall, except as the President or his representative may hereafter otherwise direct, be deemed to include such attachments thereto as are referred to in the respective proclamations or orders.SecDefinitionsection 1505(a) of title 4444 U.S.C. 1505(a). 8. . Upon its publication in the , this order shall supersede Executive Order No. 10006 of .SecFederal RegisterPrior orderOctober 9, 1948The regulations prescribed by this order shall be codified under Title 1 of the Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: .Proclamations and Executive Orders; Documents Having General Applicability and Legal Effect; Documents Required To Be Published by Congress
children.label: a .Proclamations and Executive Orders; Documents Having General Applicability and Legal Effect; Documents Required To Be Published by Congress

children.text: Presidential proclamations and Executive orders, except those not having general applicability and legal effect or effective only against Federal agencies or persons in their capacity as officers, agents, or employees thereof;
documents or classes of documents that the President may determine from time to time have general applicability and legal effect; and
documents or classes of documents that may be required so to be published by Act of Congress.

children.heading: .Documents Authorized To Be Published by Regulations; News Commentary and News Items Excluded
children.label: b .Documents Authorized To Be Published by Regulations; News Commentary and News Items Excluded
children.text: In addition to the foregoing there shall also be published in the Federal Register other documents or classes of documents authorized to be published by regulations prescribed under this chapter with the approval of the President, but news commentary or news items of any character may not be published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: .—Alternative Publication
children.label: c .—Alternative Publication
children.text: In a continuity of operations event in which the Government Publishing Office does not fulfill the publication requirements of this chapter, the Office of the Federal Register may establish an alternative method to publish the Federal Register until such time that the Government Publishing Office resumes publication.

children.heading: .Suspension of Requirements for Filing of Documents; Alternate Systems for Promulgating, Filing, or Publishing Documents; Preservation of Originals
children.label: d .Suspension of Requirements for Filing of Documents; Alternate Systems for Promulgating, Filing, or Publishing Documents; Preservation of Originals

children.text: publication of the Federal Register or filing of documents with the Office of the Federal Register is impracticable, or

under existing conditions publication in the Federal Register would not serve to give appropriate notice to the public of the contents of documents, the President may, without regard to any other provision of law, suspend all or part of the requirements of law or regulation for filing with the Office or publication in the Federal Register of documents or classes of documents.
